**Overall: PASS** — No retries needed (0/3 retries used).

### Mechanistic Path (6 nodes, 5 edges)

```
Atorvastatin
  --[decreases activity of]--> HMG-CoA reductase (HMGCR / UniProt:P04035)
  --[participates in]--> cholesterol biosynthetic process (GO:0006695)
  --[negatively regulates]--> LDL receptor (UniProt:P01130)
  --[negatively regulates]--> Lipoproteins, LDL (MESH:D008077)
  --[causes]--> Hypercholesterolemia (MESH:D006937)
```

**Mechanistic rationale:** Atorvastatin competitively inhibits HMGCR (the rate-limiting enzyme of the mevalonate/cholesterol biosynthetic pathway). Reduced HMGCR activity lowers intracellular cholesterol synthesis, which via SREBP-2 activation secondarily upregulates hepatic LDL receptor expression. The upregulated LDLR increases endocytic clearance of circulating LDL particles from the bloodstream. Reduced circulating LDL addresses the hallmark of hypercholesterolemia.

---

### PMIDs Cited

| PMID | Role |
|------|------|
| **PMID:35894149** | Statin competitive inhibition of HMG-CoA reductase; reduced mevalonate/cholesterol biosynthesis |
| **PMID:12793598** | Reduced hepatic cholesterol synthesis → secondary LDLR upregulation (SREBP mechanism) |
| **PMID:30953636** | LDLR-mediated endocytosis and clearance of circulating LDL particles |
| **PMID:27005486** | Elevated LDL as driver of hypercholesterolemia |

---
